intro:
About 460 stations are within a 30-minute commute of Tokyo Station, expanding to 848 stations within 45 minutes — one of the widest reaches of any commute destination among all 1831 stations in the Tokyo metropolitan area.

Tokyo Station is the heart of metropolitan business, where the corporate headquarters of Marunouchi meet the finance and trading firms of the Yaesu and Nihombashi areas. It is also the terminal for the Shinkansen lines, making it highly convenient for jobs involving frequent business trips or nationwide travel. Commuters here are centered on finance, trading companies, consulting, and the head offices of major manufacturers, spread across a wide age range that includes management.

The commute zone extends in every direction. To the north, the JR Keihin-Tohoku, Utsunomiya, and Takasaki lines reach Saitama; to the east, the JR Sobu Line Rapid and Keiyo Line reach Chiba; to the south, the JR Tokaido and Yokosuka lines reach Kanagawa; and to the west, the JR Chuo Line stretches the 45-minute zone toward Mitaka and Kokubunji. From the underground platforms you can also transfer on foot to the Tokyo Metro Marunouchi and Tozai lines — the Marunouchi Line toward Otemachi and Kasumigaseki, and the Tozai Line toward Nihombashi and Kayabacho — using them as one integrated network.

For keeping rent down, candidates include the Warabi and Kawaguchi direction on the JR Keihin-Tohoku Line, the Matsudo and Kita-Kogane area on the JR Joban Line, Ichikawa and Motoyawata on the JR Sobu Line Rapid, and the Shin-Urayasu and Kasai-Rinkai-Koen direction on the JR Keiyo Line. All reach Tokyo Station without transfers, and the 45-minute commute zone average of around ¥91,000 is comparatively affordable for a Marunouchi terminal.

| Area | Rent benchmark (single) | To Tokyo Sta. | Main lines |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Warabi / Kawaguchi | ¥70,000–90,000 | 25–35 min | JR Keihin-Tohoku Line |
| Matsudo | ¥70,000–90,000 | 25–30 min | JR Joban Line Rapid |
| Ichikawa / Motoyawata | ¥80,000–100,000 | 20–30 min | JR Sobu Line Rapid |
| Shin-Urayasu | ¥80,000–100,000 | 15–25 min | JR Keiyo Line |
| Nakano / Koenji | ¥90,000–110,000 | 25–35 min | JR Chuo Line |

On Kayoha, a color map in 5-minute commute-time bands gives you a full view of all 1831 stations relative to Tokyo Station, and while comparing rent benchmarks and neighborhood profiles, you can use the AI recommendation of 20 stations at once to narrow down neighborhoods that match your criteria right on the map.

faq.q: Which areas within a 30-minute commute of Tokyo Station have affordable rent?
faq.a: Candidates include the Warabi and Kawaguchi direction on the JR Keihin-Tohoku Line, the Matsudo area on the JR Joban Line, and Ichikawa and Motoyawata on the JR Sobu Line Rapid. All reach Tokyo Station without transfers in around 30 minutes, and listings below the roughly ¥90,000 benchmark are relatively easy to find.

faq.q: Which lines offer short commutes to Tokyo Station?
faq.a: The JR Tokaido, Keihin-Tohoku, Yokosuka, Chuo, Sobu Rapid, and Keiyo lines all run directly into Tokyo Station. Underground, the Tokyo Metro Marunouchi and Tozai lines are also available, so whichever direction you live in, securing a no-transfer 30-minute commute zone is relatively easy.

faq.q: What is the area around Tokyo Station like? Is it a good place to live?
faq.a: Marunouchi, Yaesu, and Nihombashi are primarily business districts undergoing redevelopment, with limited housing. Realistic options for living include the adjacent Kanda and Ningyocho areas, or the waterfront Tsukishima and Kachidoki areas beyond Ginza, from which you can commute to Marunouchi even on foot or by bicycle.

faq.q: Do singles and families prefer different areas for a Tokyo Station commute?
faq.a: Singles tend to choose convenience-focused areas like Nakano, Koenji, and Ichikawa, while families lean toward suburban areas with a stronger residential environment, such as Shin-Urayasu and Funabashi, or Musashi-Kosugi and Kawasaki on the Kanagawa side.

faq.q: Are there first-stop stations that make a seated commute to Tokyo Station easy?
faq.a: Typical options include JR Sobu Line Rapid trains starting from Chiba, Keiyo Line trains from Soga, and Chuo Line trains starting from the Takao and Otsuki direction, making a seated commute realistic even on weekday mornings. The abundance of first-stop options is one of the distinctive advantages of commuting to Tokyo Station.
